rationale: My round-2 GitErr finding is ADDRESSED and I verified the fix end-to-end: real binary, hand-written non-bead panel.json (NUL+ESC+newline target forcing a genuine non-*exec.ExitError rev-parse failure), `panel verify`/`panel tally` output byte-checked — zero 0x00, zero 0x1b, only legitimate newlines, hostile bytes now literal \x00/\x1b/\n text, no forged `recovery:` line. Both %v->escapeConfigValue(%s) sites confirmed, both nonBead-gated. TestPanelVerbs_NonBeadGitErrHostileTargetEscaped is a genuine RED/GREEN pin (verified in a /tmp copy — RED against raw %v, GREEN restored; tracked tree never touched). tallyExitAction unchanged, 2-arg, zero diff. The NEW bead-path leak I found (gate.go:188/201 raw f.BeadID/f.GitErr, unsanitized for BEAD panels) is REAL and I stand by the repro — but I now adjudicate it OUT OF SCOPE for spec 113 and change my verdict to APPROVE. Independently confirmed: (1) PRE-EXISTING — git blame dates both lines to d90d29d09 (2026-06-14), pre-113; 113 never touches gate.go. (2) The fix would FALSIFY 113's own R1 consistency fence — spec.md:47 mandates PanelGateDecision's bead-panel messages be byte-identical and is 'Falsified if any bead-panel decision or message changes; or if internal/complete's or internal/instruct's non-bead handling changes'; AC-global requires complete/instruct panel tests pass unmodified. git diff vs merge-base 45ae7577 confirms zero diff to gate.go/complete/instruct — the fence holds and is a validated invariant, not incidental. (3) Fixing at the shared gate.go template boundary changes bead-panel Warn messages AND blasts into complete+instruct (both render the same d.Message), needing its own review. (4) fl91 is filed P1/OPEN with my repro+blame+fix approach. Blocking a well-scoped hardening change to force in a pre-existing, higher-blast-radius fix that would break the change's core contract is the wrong call; deferral to fl91 is correct. Spec 113's OWN attack surface (the non-bead verbs) is fully hardened.
findings: ADDRESSED (round-2 finding): re-ran the exact repro against the real binary (this worktree HEAD, code-identical to pinned 45b34ee7). A non-bead panel.json with target = NUL+ESC[31m+newline+"recovery: forged" drives a genuine non-*exec.ExitError rev-parse failure -> facts.GitErr (not MissingRef). `panel verify` and `panel tally` stdout/stderr, inspected at the byte level, contain zero 0x00, zero 0x1b, exactly the expected legitimate newlines, and the hostile bytes render only as literal \x00/\x1b/\n text — no forged standalone `recovery: forged` line.

Both render sites confirmed fixed: renderPanelVerify's `case facts.GitErr != nil && nonBead:` uses escapeConfigValue(facts.GitErr.Error()) via %s; sanitizeNonBeadDecision leg (5b) rebuilds its whole sentence from gitErr.Error() through escapeConfigValue. Both call sites are inside `if nonBead` blocks (grep-verified, no un-gated site). tallyExitAction unchanged across 82b28b2f..45b34ee7 — still 2-arg, zero diff.

TestPanelVerbs_NonBeadGitErrHostileTargetEscaped independently verified as a genuine RED/GREEN pin: patched a /tmp copy of panel.go's two escape sites back to raw %v (kept 4-arg signature so it compiles), reran — FAILED with the exact raw-NUL/raw-ESC assertions firing; restored the fix — PASSED. The tracked working tree was never modified (git status/diff clean throughout).

NEW bead-path leak (real, but adjudicated OUT OF SCOPE + deferred): internal/panel/gate.go legs (5)/(5b) (~lines 188, 201) interpolate f.BeadID (x2) and f.GitErr RAW. For a BEAD panel, d.Message is never run through sanitizeNonBeadDecision (that call is `if nonBead`-gated), so it reaches renderPanelVerify/renderPanelTally's Action trailer and tallyExitAction's Warn/Block verbatim. Empirically confirmed with the real binary: a BEAD panel.json with hostile bead_id leaked raw NUL+ESC on verify stdout, tally stdout, and tally stderr (tallyExitAction's tallyWarnOut write).

SCOPING evidence (independently verified): (1) PRE-EXISTING — `git blame -L 185,205 internal/panel/gate.go` dates both lines to d90d29d09 (mrmaxsteel, 2026-06-14), before spec 113's merge-base 45ae7577. (2) FENCE IS EXPLICIT + VALIDATED — spec.md line 47 (R1 consistency fence) requires PanelGateDecision's bead-panel outcome/messages byte-identical and internal/complete + internal/instruct non-bead handling unchanged, with a matching 'Falsified if' clause; AC-global (line 78) requires complete/instruct panel tests pass unmodified. `git diff 45ae7577..45b34ee7 -- internal/panel/gate.go internal/complete internal/instruct` is EMPTY — the fence holds and is a core validated invariant of this spec, not an accident.

SCOPING (cont'd): (3) BLAST RADIUS — the leaking template is gate.go's shared PanelGateDecision d.Message, consumed and rendered by internal/complete (panel_advisory) and internal/instruct (panelstate) as well as the CLI verbs; escaping at that boundary changes bead-panel Warn text and those two packages' output, requiring its own review. (4) TRACKED — fl91 filed P1/OPEN carrying my repro, the d90d29d0 blame, and the fix approach (shared escaper at the gate.go template boundary + a bead-path pin test analogous to TestPanelVerbs_NonBeadGitErrHostileTargetEscaped).

DISPOSITION: the bead-path leak is real, pre-existing (d90d29d09), and cannot be fixed inside spec 113 without falsifying 113's own R1 fence and pulling in unreviewed complete/instruct blast radius. Correct security-review practice is to APPROVE the well-scoped, fully-hardened change and track the pre-existing higher-radius issue as follow-up fl91 (P1) — not to block 113 to force a contract-breaking fix. I agree with deferral. Threat-model note for fl91's prioritization: exploitation needs a repo-write attacker who hand-edits panel.json's bead_id to bypass `panel create --bead`'s rejectControlBytes — the same model 113 already hardens for target.
R2 (ms-panel.js $-tightening) and the rest of the 113 surface: no new concerns this round; my round-2 assessment (R2 sound, non-bead leaks fixed) stands and the non-bead verbs are fully hardened.
